module 1: formulate and solve the equation $ P(x) = 50 $  
the equation is $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 = 50 $.  

module 2: solve the equation $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 = 50 $  
subtract 50 from both sides: $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 70 = 0 $.  
factor: $ -2(x^3 - 1.5x^2 - 18x + 35) = 0 $.  
solve: $ x^3 - 1.5x^2 - 18x + 35 = 0 $.  
use rational root theorem: possible roots are ±1, ±5, ±7, ±35.  
test x = 5: $ 125 - 37.5 - 90 + 35 = 37.5 $.  
test x = 3: $ 27 - 13.5 - 54 + 35 = 5.5 $.  
test x = 2: $ 8 - 6 - 36 + 35 = 1 $.  
test x = 4: $ 64 - 24 - 72 + 35 = 13 $.  
test x = 7: $ 343 - 73.5 - 126 + 35 = 121.5 $.  
test x = 1: $ 1 - 1.5 - 18 + 35 = 15.5 $.  
no rational root found.  
use numerical methods: approximate solution is x ≈ 3.5.  

module 3: determine the range of years x for which $ P(x) > 60 $  
solve $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 > 60 $  
subtract 60: $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 80 > 0 $  
factor: $ -2(x^3 - 1.5x^2 - 18x + 40) > 0
